questions.text: Which 2 strategies most effectively reduce adhesion formation following laparoscopic myomectomy? Select 2 best answers.

answerOptions.text: A) Use of multiple interrupted and figure-8 sutures
C) Avoidance of dry field dissection

questions.explanation: Running sutures reduce foreign body reaction; keeping tissues moist reduces adhesion risk. Interrupted/figure-8 sutures and unsutured defects increase adhesions. Embolization isn’t a standard post-myomectomy strategy.

questions.text: Which steps reduce ureteral injury during laparoscopic hysterectomy? Select 3 best answers.

answerOptions.text: A) Ureteral stents in all cases

questions.explanation: Direct visualization via retroperitoneal dissection plus manipulator-driven cephalad traction increases the distance between ureter and operative field. Routine stents are not indicated; colpotomy avoidance is neither practical nor protective.

questions.text: Which statements best reflect known disadvantages of supracervical hysterectomy? Select 2 best answers.

answerOptions.text: A) Lower ureteral injury rates than total hysterectomy
B) Higher risk of vaginal cuff dehiscence

questions.explanation: Retained endometrium can cause ongoing cyclic bleeding; some patients later require trachelectomy.

questions.text: Which of the following are valid reasons to proceed with surgical treatment for endometrioma? Select all that apply.

answerOptions.text: D) Stable size with no symptoms

questions.explanation: Operate for refractory pain, concerning imaging, enlarging cysts, or when IVF planning is impacted. Asymptomatic stable cysts may be observed.

questions.text: What is the most common complication following laparoscopic myomectomy?
questions.explanation: Blood loss is the most frequent complication, especially with large or multiple fibroids.

questions.text: Which of the following is most associated with increased blood loss during myomectomy?

answerOptions.text: C) Number and size of fibroids

questions.explanation: Larger/more numerous fibroids → longer cases and more dissection → more bleeding.

questions.text: Which of the following techniques has been shown to decrease the risk of adhesion formation after laparoscopic myomectomy?

answerOptions.text: E) Use of cautery for hemostasis

questions.explanation: Running closure uses less material and fewer knots → less foreign body reaction.

questions.text: What structure is most at risk during uterine artery transection in laparoscopic hysterectomy?
questions.explanation: The ureter (‘water’) courses beneath the uterine artery (‘bridge’) near the cervix/vaginal cuff.

questions.text: Which surgical step is most likely to cause ureteral injury at the level of the vaginal fornix?
questions.explanation: Risk peaks near the cervix/fornix as the ureter passes under the uterine artery.

questions.text: Which strategy best reduces the risk of ureteral injury during laparoscopic hysterectomy?

answerOptions.text: A) Placing the patient in steep Trendelenburg
B) Using uterine manipulators for cephalad movement
C) Limiting visualization of the ureters to the pelvic brim
D) Avoiding use of cautery near the uterosacral ligaments
E) Delaying identification of ureters until after colpotomy

questions.explanation: Manipulator-driven cephalad traction increases the ureter–operative field distance.

questions.text: Which of the following is a contraindication to supracervical hysterectomy?

answerOptions.text: E) Desire to reduce prolapse risk

questions.explanation: Retaining a cervix with prior dysplasia is contraindicated.

questions.text: Which of the following is a known disadvantage of supracervical hysterectomy?

answerOptions.text: C) Increased risk of cuff dehiscence

questions.explanation: Residual endometrium in the cervical stump can bleed.

questions.text: Which of the following is a true statement about supracervical hysterectomy?

answerOptions.text: A) It is associated with significantly less adhesion formation
B) It provides superior long-term pelvic floor support
C) It should be considered for patients with cervical dysplasia
D) It has lower reoperation rates than total hysterectomy
E) It may result in cyclic bleeding due to residual endometrium

questions.explanation: Retained functional endometrium can lead to ongoing cyclic bleeding.

questions.text: What is the preferred surgical treatment for a symptomatic 6 cm endometrioma in a patient who has failed medical therapy?
questions.explanation: Cystectomy offers symptom relief and lower recurrence while preserving ovarian tissue.

questions.text: Which surgical technique is associated with the lowest recurrence of endometriomas?
questions.explanation: While cystectomy minimizes recurrence with ovarian preservation, oophorectomy most effectively eliminates recurrence but is reserved for select patients not desiring fertility.

questions.text: Following laparoscopic cystectomy for endometrioma, which of the following best reduces recurrence?
questions.explanation: Postoperative hormonal suppression (e.g., OCPs) reduces recurrence risk.
